name: bias-variance decomposition
description: The decomposition of expected prediction error into irreducible error σ²_ε, squared bias [Ef̂(x₀)-f(x₀)]², and variance E[f̂(x₀)-Ef̂(x₀)]². More complex models have lower bias but higher variance.

name: model complexity
description: Measure of flexibility of a model class, affecting bias-variance tradeoff.

name: degrees of freedom
description: Effective number of parameters in a model, measuring model complexity.

name: irreducible error
description: Error due to intrinsic variability in Y that cannot be reduced by any prediction method.

name: optimism of training error
description: Tendency of training error to underestimate true test error, increasing with model complexity.

name: in-sample error
description: The expected prediction error for N new response values at each of the training input points, denoted Err_in = (1/N)Σᵢ E_Y⁰[L(Yᵢ⁰, f̂(xᵢ))|T], used to measure optimism of training error.

name: out-of-sample error
description: Error measured on data not used for training.

name: expected test error
description: The expected value of test error over all possible training sets of fixed size.

name: pointwise prediction error
description: Prediction error at a specific point x₀ in the input space.

name: misclassification error
description: Probability of incorrect classification, equivalent to expected 0-1 loss.

name: posterior probability
description: Pr(G=k|X=x), probability of class k given input x.

name: nearest neighbor region
description: Set of points closer to a given training point than to any other training point, forming Voronoi tessellation.

name: euclidean distance
description: Standard distance metric ||x-x'|| used in k-nearest neighbors.

name: k-nearest neighbor boundary
description: Decision boundary formed by k-NN classifier, which becomes smoother as k increases.

name: dimension reduction
description: Techniques to reduce the dimensionality of the input space before applying prediction methods.

name: feature extraction
description: Preprocessing high-dimensional features through transformation (linear or nonlinear) before applying a learning algorithm.

name: projection method
description: Method that projects high-dimensional data onto lower-dimensional subspace.

name: least squares projection
description: Fitting by least squares viewed as projection onto a linear subspace.

name: linear smoother
description: A fitting method where fitted values are a linear function of responses, characterized by a smoother matrix independent of y.
section: 5.4.1 Degrees of Freedom and Smoother Matrices

name: hat matrix
description: The matrix H = X(XT X)^-1 XT that puts the hat on y by computing the orthogonal projection onto the column space of X.
section: 5.4.1 Degrees of Freedom and Smoother Matrices

name: effective degrees of freedom
description: For ridge regression, df(lambda) = sum of d_j^2/(d_j^2 + lambda), measuring the number of parameters effectively used in a regularized fit.

name: model selection criterion
description: Criterion for choosing among models, balancing fit and complexity.

name: training set validation
description: Using held-out data to estimate test error and select models.

name: cross-validation error
description: Estimate of test error obtained by cross-validation.

name: prior distribution
description: Distribution over function parameters representing beliefs before seeing data.

name: posterior distribution
description: In Bayesian inference, the updated distribution of parameters after observing data, computed via Bayes rule from the prior and likelihood.

name: bayesian framework
description: Approach treating parameters as random and computing posterior distributions.

name: single hidden layer neural network
description: Neural network with one layer of hidden units between inputs and outputs.

name: weight decay
description: A regularization method for neural networks analogous to ridge regression, adding an L2 penalty on the weights to the error function.

name: squared bias
description: Component of prediction error equal to squared difference between expected prediction and true function, decreases with model complexity.
section: 2.9 Model Selection and the Bias–Variance Tradeoﬀ

name: variance of prediction
description: Component of prediction error measuring variability of predictions across training sets, increases with model complexity.

name: gauss-markov theorem
description: States that the least squares estimates of parameters have the smallest variance among all linear unbiased estimates.

name: orthogonal projection
description: The projection of y onto the column space of X, minimizing the residual sum of squares.

name: qr decomposition
description: A matrix factorization that decomposes a matrix into an orthogonal matrix Q and an upper triangular matrix R, used as a numerically stable method for least squares.

name: gram-schmidt orthogonalization
description: A procedure for constructing an orthogonal basis from a set of vectors by sequential orthogonalization.
section: 3.2.3 Multiple Regression from Simple Univariate Regression

name: best subset selection
description: For each k, finds the subset of size k that gives the smallest residual sum of squares.

name: leaps and bounds procedure
description: An efficient algorithm for best subset selection that makes it feasible for p as large as 30 or 40.

name: forward stepwise selection
description: Starts with the intercept and sequentially adds the predictor that most improves the fit, producing a greedy nested sequence of models.

name: backward stepwise selection
description: Starts with the full model and sequentially deletes the predictor with the smallest Z-score (least impact on fit).

name: forward stagewise regression
description: Starts with all coefficients zero, identifies the variable most correlated with residual, computes simple linear regression coefficient of residual on that variable, and adds it to current coefficient without adjusting other variables.

name: ridge regression
description: Shrinks regression coefficients by imposing an L2 penalty on their size, controlled by parameter lambda.

name: singular value decomposition
description: A decomposition X = UDV^T where U and V are orthogonal matrices and D is diagonal, providing insight into variance structure of inputs.

name: basis pursuit
description: The signal processing name for the lasso method.

name: soft thresholding
description: Lasso's coefficient shrinkage rule that translates each coefficient by a constant, truncating at zero.

name: hard thresholding
description: An alternative to soft thresholding that sets values to zero below a threshold but leaves values above unchanged, less smooth than soft thresholding.
section: 18.1 When p is Much Bigger than N

name: elastic net penalty
description: A compromise between lasso and ridge penalties of the form λ[α||β||_1 + (1-α)||β||_2^2], encouraging correlated features to be averaged while maintaining sparsity.
section: 18.1 When p is Much Bigger than N

name: least angle regression
description: A democratic version of forward stepwise that enters 'as much' of a predictor as it deserves, moving coefficients toward least squares continuously while keeping correlations tied.

name: lar-lasso connection
description: LAR with a simple modification produces the entire lasso path: drop variables when their coefficients hit zero.

name: homotopy algorithm
description: An algorithm for computing piecewise-linear lasso path discovered by Osborne et al.

name: degrees of freedom for adaptive fit
description: df(y-hat) = sum of Cov(y-hat_i, y_i) / sigma^2, measuring how hard a model fits the data.

name: principal components regression
description: Forms derived inputs as principal components of X, then regresses y on the first M components.

name: partial least squares
description: Constructs linear combinations of inputs for regression using both X and y, weighting inputs by their univariate effect on y.

name: canonical correlation analysis
description: Finds uncorrelated linear combinations of inputs and outputs with successively maximized correlations.

name: reduced-rank regression
description: Regression method that constrains coefficient matrix to have low rank, borrowing strength among multiple responses via CCA truncation.

name: curds and whey
description: A shrinkage method for multiple outputs that shrinks canonical variates between X and Y, a smooth version of reduced rank regression.

name: incremental forward stagewise regression
description: Forward stagewise with small step size epsilon, updating coefficient of most correlated variable by epsilon*sign(correlation) repeatedly.

name: infinitesimal forward stagewise regression
description: The limiting procedure as step size epsilon approaches zero in incremental forward stagewise, also called FS0 or L2boost.

name: l2boost
description: Another name for infinitesimal forward stagewise regression due to its connections to boosting.

name: dantzig selector
description: Minimizes L1 norm of coefficients subject to maximum absolute correlation of residuals with predictors being bounded.

name: grouped lasso
description: Lasso variant for pre-defined groups of predictors that encourages sparsity at both group and individual levels using group-wise L2 norms.

name: relaxed lasso
description: Two-stage lasso: first run lasso to select predictors, then apply lasso again only to selected predictors with separate penalty parameter to reduce bias.

name: scad penalty
description: Smoothly clipped absolute deviation penalty that shrinks large coefficients less severely than lasso to reduce bias.

name: adaptive lasso
description: Weighted lasso penalty using weights w_j = 1/|beta-hat_j|^nu from OLS, yielding consistent parameter estimates while maintaining convexity.

name: pathwise coordinate descent
description: Algorithm for computing lasso solution by fixing penalty parameter and optimizing successively over each parameter while holding others fixed.

name: z-score for regression coefficients
description: The standardized coefficient z_j = beta-hat_j / se(beta-hat_j) used for hypothesis testing, distributed as t_{N-p-1} under null hypothesis.
section: 3.2 Linear Regression Models and Least Squares

name: f-statistic for nested models
description: Test statistic for comparing nested linear models, measuring change in RSS per additional parameter normalized by error variance estimate.

name: variance-covariance matrix of least squares estimates
description: Var(beta-hat) = (X^T X)^{-1} sigma^2, giving the sampling variability of parameter estimates.

name: multiple regression coefficient interpretation
description: The jth coefficient represents the additional contribution of x_j on y after x_j has been adjusted for all other predictors.
section: 3.2.3 Multiple Regression from Simple Univariate Regression

name: multicollinearity
description: When predictors are highly correlated, coefficients become unstable with high variance, as small residual vectors lead to imprecise estimates.
section: 3.2.3 Multiple Regression from Simple Univariate Regression

name: bias-variance tradeoff in model selection
description: Shrinkage and selection methods sacrifice some bias to reduce variance, potentially improving overall prediction accuracy.

name: mean squared error decomposition
description: MSE = variance + (bias)^2, showing tradeoff between variance and bias in estimation.

name: aic criterion
description: A criterion for model selection that accounts for the number of parameters fit, used to choose tuning parameters.

name: universal approximator
description: A class of models that can approximate any continuous function arbitrarily well (e.g., projection pursuit regression with enough terms).

name: l1 arc length
description: The total L1 distance traveled along a coefficient path, summing L1 norms of coefficient changes at each step.

name: piecewise-linear solution path
description: Property of LAR and lasso where coefficient profiles are piecewise linear as tuning parameter varies, enabling efficient computation.

name: soft-thresholding operator
description: An operator S(t, λ) = sign(t)(|t|−λ)+ used in coordinate descent for lasso, which shrinks coefficients toward zero by the amount λ and sets them to zero if below the threshold.

name: warm start
description: An initialization technique where the solution from a previous optimization problem is used as the starting point for a new similar problem, commonly used in pathwise optimization algorithms.

name: cholesky decomposition
description: A matrix decomposition that factors a positive-definite matrix into the product of a lower triangular matrix and its transpose, used in least squares fitting.

name: indicator response matrix
description: A matrix representation of categorical responses where each class is coded as a separate binary indicator variable, forming an N × K matrix for K classes.
section: 4.2 Linear Regression of an Indicator Matrix

name: masking in classification
description: A phenomenon in linear regression for classification where one class becomes dominated by others and is never predicted, especially when K ≥ 3 classes are arranged in certain configurations.
section: 4.2 Linear Regression of an Indicator Matrix

name: class-conditional density
description: The probability density function of the input features X conditional on belonging to a specific class k, denoted fk(x).

name: linear discriminant function
description: A linear function δk(x) = x^T Σ^(-1)μk - (1/2)μk^T Σ^(-1)μk + log πk used in LDA to classify observations by finding the maximum discriminant value.

name: pooled covariance matrix
description: A weighted average of the within-class covariance matrices used in LDA when assuming a common covariance across all classes.

name: quadratic discriminant function
description: A discriminant function that includes quadratic terms in x, arising when different classes have different covariance matrices in Gaussian classification.

name: regularized discriminant analysis
description: A method that shrinks the within-covariance estimate toward its diagonal: ˆΣ(γ) = γˆΣ + (1-γ)diag(ˆΣ), overcoming singularity issues when p >> N.
section: 18.1 When p is Much Bigger than N

name: sphering the data
description: A transformation that rescales data so that its covariance matrix becomes the identity matrix, often done using X* ← D^(-1/2) U^T X where Σ = UDU^T.

name: reduced-rank lda
description: A dimension reduction technique in LDA that projects data onto a lower-dimensional subspace spanned by the class centroids, preserving classification information while enabling visualization.

name: fisher's linear discriminant
description: A method that finds linear combinations of features that maximize the ratio of between-class variance to within-class variance, providing optimal low-dimensional projections for classification.

name: rayleigh quotient
description: The ratio a^T Ba / a^T Wa used in Fisher's discriminant analysis, measuring the between-class to within-class variance ratio for a linear combination.

name: discriminant coordinates
description: The vectors aℓ that maximize the Rayleigh quotient successively, providing optimal directions for discriminating between classes.

name: between-class covariance
description: The covariance matrix B measuring the spread of class means around the overall mean, used in Fisher's discriminant analysis.

name: within-class covariance
description: The pooled covariance matrix W measuring the variation of observations within each class around their class means.

name: logit transformation
description: A monotone transformation log[p/(1−p)] that maps probabilities from [0,1] to the real line, used in logistic regression to model posterior probabilities.

name: log-odds
description: The logarithm of the odds ratio, equal to the logit transformation, representing the linear component in logistic regression models.

name: score equations
description: The system of equations formed by setting the derivatives of the log-likelihood to zero, solved to find maximum likelihood estimates.

name: hessian matrix
description: The matrix of second derivatives of a function, used in Newton-Raphson optimization to determine the curvature and update direction.
